lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.reference: Lax & Friedrichs (1954), Comm. Pure Appl. Math. 7(1):159-193 (LF flux); src/operators/flux_1d.jl `flux_1d` ArrayOp on `CubedSphereGrid` (imperative reference). The Cartesian core lives at `discretizations/finite_volume/lax_friedrichs_flux.json`; this rule is the cubed-sphere face-stagger ξ-direction wrapper that pairs the Cartesian LF flux algebra with cubed_sphere selectors and a per-face Courant binding precomputed by the grid accessor. The η-direction sibling lives in `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta.json`.
lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.notes: ξ-direction Lax-Friedrichs numerical flux at a UEdge interface on the gnomonic equidistant cubed sphere. Uses cell-center scalars `$q` from the two cells flanking the ξ-face (offsets -1, 0 along ξ at cell_center stagger) and a face-staggered Courant `$c` (offset 0 along ξ at u_edge stagger). The closed-form upwind-blended stencil F = (c+|c|)/2·q_west + (c-|c|)/2·q_east mirrors `lax_friedrichs_flux` (Cartesian 1D) coefficient-for-coefficient — only the selector kind / stagger and the offset convention differ. Cross-panel ghost extension at the western u_edge of panel p (face index i=1) and the eastern u_edge (i=Nc+1) is resolved by the cubed_sphere grid accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl); selectors carry no `panel` field per SELECTOR_KINDS.md decision #13. Panel-boundary distance handling for the Courant precomputation (grid.dist_xi_bnd ↔ grid.dist_xi at interior interfaces) is encapsulated in the accessor's `_get_courant_xi` / `compute_courant_numbers_arrayop` (src/operators/flux_1d.jl) — by the time this rule sees `$c` it is a single face-staggered array indexed by (p,i,j), with boundary distances already factored in.
lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.panel_handling: lives in the cubed_sphere grid accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl); the rule references in-panel offsets only. Selectors do NOT carry a `panel` field — cross-panel ghost extension and (for vector quantities) basis rotation happen below the rule layer.

metric_bindings.comment: The cubed-sphere LF flux algebra carries no metric coefficients of its own — the Courant `c` is the only per-face binding, and the cubed_sphere accessor precomputes it including panel-boundary distance handling. Reported here for cross-reference with the imperative `compute_courant_numbers` / `compute_courant_numbers_arrayop` (src/operators/flux_1d.jl).
metric_bindings.c: face-staggered Courant number c[p,i,j] = vel[p,i,j]·dt / dist_xi[p,i,j] at u_edge stagger; interior interfaces use grid.dist_xi (cell-center to cell-center distance), panel-boundary interfaces (i=1, i=Nc+1) use grid.dist_xi_bnd (full-cell-width distance to the neighbor-panel ghost cell). Precomputed by the accessor and consumed here as a single face-staggered binding.

flux_form.comment: Algebraic identity: F_{i+1/2} = (c+|c|)/2·q_west + (c-|c|)/2·q_east = max(c,0)·q_west + min(c,0)·q_east. `q_west` is the cell at ξ-offset -1 from the face (cell_center stagger), `q_east` at offset 0; `c` is the face's own Courant at u_edge offset 0. Upwind cell selection is encoded directly via `abs` — no caller-side branching on sign(c). Identical algebra to `lax_friedrichs_flux` with selector kind / stagger swapped.

lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.composition_hint: Caller precomputes face-staggered Courants `c[p,i,j] = u_face[p,i,j]·dt / dist[p,i,j]` (use `compute_courant_numbers_arrayop` in `src/operators/flux_1d.jl` until the boundary_policy schema feature lands and lets the accessor publish c directly), then applies this rule at every ξ-interface. Cell-centred tendency follows from FV divergence: T[p,i,j] = -(F[p,i+1,j]·dx[p,i+1,j] - F[p,i,j]·dx[p,i,j]) / area[p,i,j] — composes with `divergence_arakawa_c` (or, on cubed_sphere, the metric-aware FV divergence on a C-grid). Same first-order dissipative profile as the Cartesian core; retained as the cubed-sphere debugging / oracle scheme. Production transport on the cubed sphere should use the higher-order PPM-based flux (tracked at dsc-r1i, blocked on the same schema infrastructure).
lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.schema_notes: Face-stagger schema features used: (a) `cubed_sphere_stagger` enum (decision #17, pinned by dsc-247), (b) per-cell grid-accessor metric bindings on cubed_sphere (decision #18). The face-staggered Courant `$c` is consumed via the `reads` block at u_edge stagger — analogous to how `fv3_sinsg_flux_xi.json` consumes its face velocity `v` and how the Cartesian `lax_friedrichs_flux` consumes the slope-ratio-style scalar `$c`. Boundary_policy metadata (per dsc-35x follow-up) is NOT yet expressed in the rule schema — instead, panel-boundary distance handling lives in the cubed_sphere grid accessor's Courant precomputation, and this rule consumes the precomputed `$c` opaquely. When the boundary_policy schema feature lands, this rule's metric_bindings block should be extended with an explicit `boundary_policy` reference to grid.dist_xi_bnd; a follow-up bead is tracked off dsc-0fd.
limitations: Output is at u_edge stagger (ξ-direction face flux only); the η-direction sibling is `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta`. There is no single multi-axis cubed_sphere LF flux rule because emits_location differs by axis (u_edge vs v_edge); a grid_dispatch / multi-axis rule form is a follow-up shared with dsc-r1i.
PPM-based cubed-sphere flux-form transport (`flux_1d_ppm_arrayop` in `src/operators/flux_1d.jl`) is a separate higher-order scheme tracked at dsc-r1i; not encoded here.

Same-direction panel-boundary flux matching (`_match_boundary_fluxes_xi!` in `src/operators/flux_1d.jl`) is a downstream conservation step performed AFTER the rule produces face fluxes; encoding the matcher in the schema (parallel to a post-stencil clamp like the FV3 monotonicity guard) is a follow-up shared with `ppm_edge_cubed_sphere`.

Rotated-boundary flux matching (`_match_rotated_boundary_fluxes!`, applies to ξ↔η panel connections) is a coupled-axis post-processing step that cannot be expressed by either of the per-axis sibling rules in isolation; remains in the imperative wrapper until a multi-axis post-processing schema feature lands.
